scores.correctness_notes: Correctly covers the change/task model structure and Python ops client methods (wait_change, get_change, get_changes). However, misses several key approaches from the gold standard: CLI commands (pebble changes, pebble tasks), direct REST API polling/blocking wait endpoints, change-update notices via long-polling, and state persistence to $PEBBLE/.pebble.state. The Python ops coverage is accurate but the response is incomplete relative to the full gold standard.
scores.hallucination_notes: none — all referenced APIs (container.wait_change, container.get_change, container.get_changes, ops.pebble.ChangeStatus, ops.pebble.ChangeState.IN_PROGRESS, ops.pebble.ChangeError, ExecProcess.wait) appear to be real ops library APIs. The change status state machine (DO→DOING→DONE, ERROR, ABORT/UNDO/UNDOING, HOLD, WAIT) also appears accurate.
